stories.title: Trump Says He Was Noncommittal on Taiwan, to Decide on Sale Soon
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said he made no commitment to Chinese President Xi Jinping over Taiwan, and would make a decision soon over a $14 billion arms deal with the island.

stories.title: Bonds, stocks and precious metals slump as inflation fears mount, silver falls 7%
stories.summary: Mounting inflation fears, geopolitical tension and ongoing uncertainty around the Iran war has prompted selling pressure across asset classes.

stories.title: Republicans Fumble on Affordability as Voters’ Worries Mount
stories.summary: The Republican-led Congress, faced with deepening economic discontent exacerbated by President Donald Trump’s Iran war, is struggling to find consensus on any plan to address the cost of living with less than six months before midterm elections.

stories.title: FirstFT: Iran dominates second day of Trump-Xi talks
stories.summary: Also in today’s newsletter: SpaceX’s sci-fi business strategy, and how the oil futures market works

stories.title: Winners and Losers From Trump and Xi’s Two-Day Beijing Summit
stories.summary: Donald Trump got the pageantry he craved during his trip to China. But the US president concluded the summit largely where he began, receiving little help from his “friend” Xi Jinping in dealing with a messy war in Iran and a challenging political climate at home.

stories.title: AI-Hacking Threat Pushes $130 Billion Crypto Sector to the Brink
stories.summary: The crypto hacks came a little over two weeks apart in April, netting the attackers almost $600 million in total while triggering an investor exodus from one major platform and causing another to fail.

stories.title: Pollution From Coal Is Hurting Global Solar Output, Study Finds
stories.summary: Pollution from coal-fired power plants that can block sunlight is significantly cutting global solar output, and potentially causing an overestimation of climate progress, according to a new study.

stories.title: Dollar Rallies Toward Best Week Since March on Fed Hike View
stories.summary: The dollar is heading for its best week in two months after US data showed price pressures that could push the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates over the next year.

stories.title: China and US ‘feel very similar about Iran’, Trump says
stories.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping says ‘milestone’ summit has forged a ‘new bilateral relationship’

stories.title: Trump leaves China after talks dominated by trade, oil and Taiwan
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump traveled to China this week for two days of meetings with Chinese President Xi Jinping, after a year of heightened trade tensions.

stories.title: Kioxia Preps US Listing After Riding AI Boom to Big Profit
stories.summary: Kioxia Holdings Corp., the world’s best-performing major stock this year, said it would list its shares in the US as it reaps the benefits of a global memory chip shortage that’s ratcheted up prices of the vital component.

stories.title: Putin to Visit Beijing Only Days After Trump Departs, SCMP Says
stories.summary: Russian leader Vladimir Putin will visit Beijing on May 20, the South China Morning Post reported, which would be days after President Xi Jinping’s summit in the Chinese capital with his US counterpart Donald Trump.

stories.title: Robot Makers Rule Hottest Asia Stock Trades on Physical AI Boom
stories.summary: Robotics has become one of the hottest stock themes in Asia as the artificial intelligence trade broadens beyond the region’s chipmakers.

stories.title: Trump Says China Offered Help With Iran Negotiations
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump signaled China is willing to support negotiations with Iran, as he pushes for a diplomatic resolution to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Trump is visiting his Chinese counterpart President Xi Jinping in Beijing. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Streeting backs Burnham’s by-election run
stories.summary: Labour set to approve Greater Manchester mayor standing in Makerfield

stories.title: Berro: Fed Should Communicate On-Hold Path
stories.summary: Kelsey Berro, Fixed Income Portfolio Manager at JPMorgan Asset Management, joins Bloomberg’s Vonnie Quinn on “Bloomberg Brief” to break down the ongoing selloff in the global bond market and discuss the Fed’s potential course of action in an uncertain trading environment. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: India Braces for More Fuel Hikes As Iran War Forces Modi’s Hand
stories.summary: India’s consumers — hit with the first fuel price hike in four years — will likely face more such rises in the near term, as the government grapples with the economic impact of a months-long conflict in the Persian Gulf.

stories.title: European stocks slide as inflation fears return; Starmer faces leadership challenge
stories.summary: Shortly after 11:00 a.m. in London, the pan-European Stoxx 600 index was seen trading down 1.3%.

stories.title: Chinese EV makers hunt for idled plants in Europe as they expand global influence
stories.summary: Chinese electric vehicle (EV) makers, from BYD to Leapmotor, are voraciously seeking out manufacturing assets in Europe as the global energy crisis creates surging demand for battery-powered vehicles. Their pursuit of idled European assembly facilities owned by big names like Stellantis and Volkswagen is disrupting the pecking order in the global automotive industry, as the Chinese carmakers’ desi

stories.title: India’s Gold Demand Slows to Trickle on Tighter Trade Rules
stories.summary: India’s gold imports are slowing to a trickle as banks and bullion traders grapple with new restrictions aimed at shoring up a weak rupee battered by the Middle East war.

stories.title: Xi-Trump Meet Gives China Markets Little Reason to Change Course
stories.summary: Chinese stocks halted their rally and the yuan held steady as the high profile summit between Xi Jinping and Donald Trump delivered a predictable script. The absence of substantiative breakthroughs in trade or diplomacy suggests investors are likely to stick with the status quo.

stories.title: China braced for heavy rains as UN hails its role as ‘global climate leader’
stories.summary: Flood warnings issued for centre and east, and more than a month’s average rain forecast over four days in south

stories.title: Trump Projects Confidence on Iran War While China Stays Cautious
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the US and China share common goals for ending the Iran war, while Beijing struck a measured tone in urging further diplomacy.

stories.title: Singapore Airlines to add flights in win from Iran war disruption
stories.summary: Airline gains passengers from rivals while hit related to Air India stake erodes annual profit

stories.title: Burnham's Move Escalates Labour Revolt Against Starmer
stories.summary: Mayor of Manchester Andy Burnham secured a path to challenge Keir Starmer for the UK prime minister’s job, after a Labour Party Member of Parliament quit his seat in the House of Commons. Burnham said he would seek permission from Labour’s National Executive Committee to run for the freed-up seat in a by-election. Bloomberg's Alex Morgan reports from Downing Street. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Chinese margin traders raise wagers to record high as tech stock rally in full swing
stories.summary: China’s stock investors boosted their leveraged bets to an all-time high, helping fuel a record-setting run on technology stocks amid easing geopolitical tensions and earnings resilience. The outstanding value of margin trading on the mainland’s exchanges reached 2.86 trillion yuan (US$420.8 billion) on Thursday, rising to a record for a seventh straight day, according to the data by China Securit

stories.title: The great global bond freakout
stories.summary: Plus, crypto’s not money

stories.title: Euan Blair’s Multiverse hits $2.1bn valuation in AI workforce training push
stories.summary: Education technology start-up raises $70mn in its first fundraising since 2022

stories.title: Adtek Files for Hong Kong IPO, Adding to Chinese AI Listings
stories.summary: Shenzhen Adtek Technology Co. has filed for an initial public offering in Hong Kong, adding to a wave of listings by Chinese companies related to data centers and artificial intelligence.

stories.title: OpenAI May Raise More Capital as Compute Crunch Deepens, CFO Says
stories.summary: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar says the ChatGPT maker may raise more capital even after completing what she described as the largest private fundraising round ever. She speaks with Caroline Hyde on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: OpenAI May Raise More Money as Compute Crunch Deepens, CFO Says
stories.summary: OpenAI Chief Financial Officer Sarah Friar said the ChatGPT maker may raise more capital even after completing what she described as the largest private fundraising round ever, as the company races to secure computing power to meet surging AI demand.

stories.title: India Hikes Fuel Prices For The First Time in Four Years
stories.summary: India’s state-run refiners have raised fuel prices for the first time in four years, as New Delhi yields to crude prices pushed higher by the war in Iran, and to the resulting financial pressures battering its processors.

stories.title: Alphabet Sells Biggest Yen Bond on Record by Foreign Issuer
stories.summary: Alphabet Inc. sold ¥576.5 billion ($3.6 billion) of bonds in the biggest ever yen deal by a non-Japanese company as competition to fund data centers and AI infrastructure intensifies.

stories.title: Anthropic Spat With US Emerges as Risk Factor for Figma, Others
stories.summary: Anthropic PBC’s legal dispute with the US government over whether the company’s artificial intelligence models will be banned from federal agencies has emerged as a financial threat to other businesses.

stories.title: Cramer backs Nvidia selling AI chips in China — but says the stock can thrive either way
stories.summary: CNBC’s Jim Cramer said Nvidia should be allowed to sell AI chips in China, arguing it is better to keep Chinese companies reliant on American technology.

stories.title: Disney Digs In Against Pressure From Trump’s FCC
stories.summary: The company is supporting ABC in a more spirited way than it did last year

stories.title: Family investors turn to old-economy businesses like dealerships and fisheries to avoid AI disruption
stories.summary: While car dealerships and fisheries are less eye-catching than high-flying AI startups, they offer stability and cash flow that's attractive to family offices.

stories.title: Trump Leaves China With New Focus on Trade, Rare Earths
stories.summary: President Donald Trump heads home after a two day summit with China's Xi Jinping in Beijing. Bloomberg's Annmarie reports on what they've accomplished and what's next. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Ecobank Seeks Yuan Link to Trim Dollar Dominance in Africa Trade
stories.summary: Pan-African lender Ecobank Transnational Inc. is exploring ways to facilitate direct yuan payments for its clients trading with China as it seeks to boost ties with the continent’s biggest trading partner and reduce reliance on the dollar.

stories.title: Burns on Key Takeaways From the Trump-Xi Summit
stories.summary: Nicholas Burns, former US Ambassador to China, joins Bloomberg’s Vonnie Quinn on “Bloomberg Brief” to discuss US-China relations as President Donald Trump and President Xi Jinping wrap up a two-day summit in Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Magyar Opens Orban’s Office to Unpick Hungary’s Illiberal Legacy
stories.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar is opening Viktor Orban’s offices to the public to dismantle the symbols of his predecessor’s rule as part of efforts to tackle costly building projects around Buda castle.

stories.title: Cerebras stock falls after blockbuster IPO debut — here's what's happening
stories.summary: Cerebras Systems' shares were lower in early trading Friday, after its blockbuster stock market debut on Thursday.

stories.title: Gilts fall as traders brace for Burnham to challenge Starmer

stories.title: Bond Investors Can’t Escape the Relentless Pressure From Oil Prices

stories.title: RBC’s Calvasina Says 5% Yield Would Challenge US Stock Bulls
stories.summary: Bullish calls on US stocks will be challenged if benchmark Treasury yields hit 5%, a level that usually depresses price-to-earnings ratios, according to Lori Calvasina, head of US equity strategy at RBC Capital Markets LLC.

stories.title: BofA’s Hartnett Says Stock Market Ripe for Profit Taking in June
stories.summary: The stock market is ripe for profit-taking in early June due to investors crowding into equities and rising inflation risks, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

stories.title: Adani Group Revives Plan to Seek $1 Billion With Dollar Bond
stories.summary: Indian conglomerate Adani Group is reviving plans to seek about $1 billion through a US-listed dollar-denominated bond, according to people familiar with the matter, a move reflecting its intent to return to the US financing market as its legal troubles there ease.

stories.title: Trump Announces Plans for Statue Garden in Potomac River Park
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said he would install his proposed sculpture garden of “American Heroes” in West Potomac Park, a stretch near the National Mall that includes the Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial.

stories.title: UAE fast tracks second West-East oil pipeline to bypass Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The second pipeline project comes as global energy supplies remain under pressure and flows through the Strait of Hormuz remain severely limited

stories.title: HSBC Drags Feet on $4 Billion Private Credit Investment Effort
stories.summary: HSBC Holdings Plc is yet to make material headway in plans to invest as much as $4 billion into its asset manager’s private credit funds, according to a spokesperson.

stories.title: Trump-Xi summit: the 3 big takeaways from historic meeting in Beijing
stories.summary: The two-day meeting wrapped up Friday, setting the tone for further U.S.-China talks this year.

stories.title: Trump Departs Beijing After Highly Choreographed Summit
stories.summary: President Trump heads home from Beijing after a highly choreographed summit with Xi Jinping where their friendliness was on full display but yielded few deals or major breakthroughs. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: India Sees Monsoon Rains Starting Early, Aiding Crop Sowing
stories.summary: India expects its monsoon rains to arrive early this year, offering some relief for farmers who rely on the seasonal showers for timely sowing of crops like rice and soybeans.

stories.title: These Memory Stocks Are Getting Cheaper Despite Scorching Rally
stories.summary: Insatiable demand for memory chips has some of the industry’s top stocks defying the market’s math, becoming cheaper to own even as their share prices soar to nosebleed levels.

stories.title: UAE Tried in Vain to Get Saudis to Coordinate on Iran Response
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates tried to persuade neighboring states including Saudi Arabia and Qatar to take part in a coordinated military response to Iran’s strikes and was left frustrated when they refused, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.title: Xi Hails ‘Landmark’ Summit as Trump Departs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump's two-day visit to China has come to an end. President Xi Jinping hailed the results of the summit and touted an agreement on a new relationship for the countries, projecting optimism despite unresolved tensions and limited deals announced so far. Bloomberg's Minmin Low reports from Beijing. (Source: Bloomberg)

He speaks with Bloomberg TV's Jennifer Zabasajja at the Africa CEO Forum in Kigali. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Gilts Slump as Investors Brace for Burnham Challenge to Starmer
stories.summary: UK government bonds tumbled after Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham secured a pathway to potentially challenge Keir Starmer for the prime minister’s job, threatening a fresh bout of political instability that investors fear could result in more expansive fiscal policy.

stories.title: Geely, Xiaomi lead the pack: battery EVs top China sales on rising oil costs
stories.summary: Surging oil prices are prompting Chinese buyers to shift to battery electric vehicles (BEVs), which dominated April sales rankings by taking nine out of the top 10 spots – a new high in China’s electrification drive.
